note: Sub-event narrative texts. These are spawned by specific crisis event choices. Smaller than crisis events — brief Colony Journal entries or optional dialogue cards only.

journal_entry.title: Day 28–30 — Vasquez Transmission (Recorded)
journal_entry.body:
Vasquez came through on channel 7 this afternoon. It was the first time since the counter-offer in Week 2. He said he'd used the eastern tunnel survey data — he didn't say how, but the numbers he cited were specific enough that he'd clearly read the whole thing. He said the coordinates I gave him led his salvage teams to a sealed maintenance corridor that gave Grid-9 access to three functioning water recyclers they'd been cut off from for six months.

He didn't say thank you, exactly. He said: 'The intelligence was worth more than the engineers would have been.' That's as close as Vasquez gets.

He also said he's been tracking something in the northern broadcast spectrum. He mentioned a coordinate — I wrote it down — and said his communications technician thought it might be a structured response to automated Syndicate emergency signals. He brought it up like it was a minor footnote. I'm not sure he understood what he was giving me.

He signed off before I could ask more. The coordinate is in the log. MERIDIAN confirmed it matches the signal they detected two days ago.

sub_events.location: Colony Journal — optional interaction, fires once only
sub_events.note: If taken before Day 33: rook_alive stays TRUE. If not taken before Day 33: rook_alive = FALSE permanently on Day 33.
sub_events.prompt_text: Rook hasn't spoken directly to you since the decision on Day 24. He's still working. He's still here. There's a moment, in the corridor outside the operations room, where you could stop and say something. You don't have to. The window closes on Day 33.

choices.text: Stop. Acknowledge what he did and why you stopped it.

outcome_journal_entry.title: Reconciliation — Day [FIRES ON DAY TAKEN]
outcome_journal_entry.body:
I stopped Rook in the corridor outside the operations room this morning. He didn't make it easy — he had that particular stillness he gets when he's decided not to be the one to close a distance — but he stopped.

I told him the decision on Day 24 was about the risk, not about what he'd built. That the salvage work he'd been doing since Day 8 was the reason Grid-7 had the materials we did in the first two weeks. That I stopped the militia because I couldn't afford to lose him, not because I thought he was wrong.

He looked at me for a moment. Then he said: 'You could have said that on Day 24.' He's right. I could have.

He's still here. He went back to work. I think that's what a reconciliation looks like in a place like this — not a resolution, but a decision to keep working in the same direction.

choices.text: [Do not take this action before Day 33]

outcome_journal_entry.title: Day 33 — Something Between Them Closed
outcome_journal_entry.body:
Something between them closed. That is the most accurate way I have to describe what happened. There was a window — I knew it was there — and I did not go through it, and on Day 33 I checked the operations log and Rook's name had been removed from the active worker roster by his own hand sometime during the night.

He filed the paperwork correctly. His inventory manifest is complete and filed. He did not leave without making sure the records were in order. That is probably the most Rook thing he could have done.

I did not find out where he went. I did not look.

choices.mechanical_outcome: rook_alive = FALSE permanently. Cannot be reversed after Day 33.

sub_events.spawned_by: rooks_militia — Option A (sanction) + Med Clinic not upgraded before Day 33

mechanical_effect.description: Two militia members injured in a tunnel collapse. Temporarily pulled from available workers. Rook himself is fine.

journal_entry.title: Day [30–33] — Tunnel Collapse, East Salvage Corridor
journal_entry.body:
Two of Rook's team caught a partial tunnel collapse in the east salvage corridor this afternoon — Devi and the older one, Carrow. Rook got them both out. Devi has a broken arm. Carrow has a concussion and two cracked ribs. Neither is critical. Both are out of rotation for three days minimum, which puts available workers down temporarily.

Rook came to report it himself. He was covered in dust and he was calm and he gave me the incident breakdown in the same format as the official salvage reports — location, cause, injuries, recovery estimate. He had clearly written it in his head on the walk over. I told him the Med Clinic could handle it. He said he knew. He left.

He's fine. The two workers will recover. The east corridor is now flagged as unstable. This is what the risk looked like when it happened. It was smaller than I feared and it was still real.

sub_events.spawned_by: the_last_broadcast — conditional on Archive Hall built AND MERIDIAN trusted
sub_events.location: Colony Journal — auto-fires as part of The Last Broadcast event resolution
sub_events.note: Required narrative context for The Signal secret ending. Does not display if Archive Hall not built or MERIDIAN not trusted.

journal_entry.title: Day 28 — Signal (Secondary Layer, Last Broadcast)
journal_entry.body:
MERIDIAN flagged something in the Syndicate broadcast that wasn't in the primary data layer.

Structured signal. Northern origin — direction confirmed, distance not calculable from our position. The signal pattern is deliberate. It responded to the Syndicate broadcast at specific intervals that don't match any automated emergency protocol MERIDIAN has on record. MERIDIAN cross-referenced the entire Syndicate signal library. No match.

This means something was listening to the broadcast and chose to answer it. The response is not noise. It is not an automated loop. It is — MERIDIAN's words — 'a signal that knows it is a signal.'

Vasquez mentioned a northern coordinate in his last transmission. MERIDIAN confirms the bearing is consistent.

I don't know what to do with this. I've written it in the log because MERIDIAN told me to write things down that might matter later, and I think this might matter later. Right now there's a storm in six days and eight hundred people who need the Hydroponic Bays shielded. The signal can wait.

I'm going to remember it's there.

sub_events.trigger_condition: deserters_option_b_taken == true AND morale < 30 at lockdown end (Day 10)

building_damage.damage_level: shifted to damaged sprite (30% output until repaired)
building_damage.repair_mechanic: player must spend Materials to restore — does not auto-recover

journal_entry.title: Day 10 — Unrest in the West Corridor
journal_entry.body:
The lockdown ended at 0600 and by 0800 there was a disturbance in the west corridor. Nobody was killed. Two security personnel injured. One building took damage — equipment pulled from its housing, fixtures broken. The people who did it are back in their section now.

I could write this up in a way that makes it sound more contained than it was. What it was: people who were told they couldn't leave, in a place where the conditions that made them want to leave haven't changed, expressing that in the only way left available to them. The lockdown held the numbers. It did not hold the tension. The tension found its exit anyway.

The damaged building needs Materials to repair. Morale dropped further. I kept everyone here. I'm not sure everyone being here is the same as everyone being fine.
